title: Rousing Vietnamese Potatoes Che Ba Mau
description: A crispy, grass green Vietnamese inspired potato dish that energizes with fresh herbs and a touch of umami, perfect for a quick snack.
instructions: Fill a deep skillet with 2 tbsp toasted sesame oil and heat to 180°C.
While oil warms, toss cubed charlotte potatoes with 1 tsp sea salt and 1 tsp chili flakes.
Once oil is hot, add potatoes in a single layer and fry for 3-4 minutes, turning occasionally until golden and crisp.
Remove potatoes with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
In a bowl, whisk together 1 cup chopped cilantro, 1 cup chopped mint, 1 cup chopped basil, 2 tbsp lime juice, 1 tsp grated ginger, 1 tbsp fish sauce, and 2 tbsp chopped scallion.
Transfer the fried potatoes to the bowl, pour the herb dressing over them, and toss gently to coat.
Sprinkle extra fresh herbs on top and serve immediately.
Optional: squeeze a few drops of lime before serving.
